column1: # $1 in fix field is replaced with the first regex match group capture

column3: Port $1 is already in use by another process
column5: Another process grabbed this port first. killport finds and kills it safely.

column3: Missing Node.js dependency — node_modules is incomplete or corrupt
column5: package.json lists a dependency that isn't in node_modules. Re-install to fix.

column3: Node.js / npm is not installed
column5: Install Node.js from https://nodejs.org or with: nvm install --lts

column3: No package.json in current directory — wrong folder
column5: You need to run npm commands from the project root where package.json lives.

column3: OpenSSL version incompatibility with your Node.js version
column5: Node 17+ changed OpenSSL defaults. This env var restores compatibility.

column3: Python module '$1' is not installed
column5: The script imported '$1' but it's not in your Python environment.

column3: pip is missing from this Python environment
column5: pip isn't available — this bootstraps it from the stdlib.

column3: requirements.txt not found in current directory
column5: You ran pip install -r but requirements.txt doesn't exist here.

column3: Python 3 is not installed or not on PATH
column5: Install Python 3 from https://python.org or: sudo apt install python3

column3: Not inside a git repository
column5: This directory hasn't been initialized as a git repo. git init creates one.

column3: Working tree is clean — nothing staged or modified
column5: All files are already committed. Use gstatus to see the current state.

column3: Remote branch doesn't exist yet
column5: First push of a new branch needs --set-upstream to create it on the remote.

column3: Merge conflicts detected — manual resolution required
column5: Open conflicting files, look for <<<<<<< markers, resolve, then gsave "fix conflicts".

column3: Git authentication failed — SSH key or credential issue
column5: Check: ssh -T git@github.com. If using HTTPS, re-enter credentials or switch to SSH.

column2: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon
column3: Docker daemon is not running
column5: The Docker engine isn't started. This command starts it.

column3: Docker port conflict — host port in use
column5: A container or process already has this port. See what's running with docker ps.

column3: Missing execute permission or wrong file ownership
column5: The file exists but the OS won't run it without execute permission.

column3: Script is not executable
column5: Add execute permission to run this file directly.

column3: '$1' is not installed or not on PATH
column5: The shell can't find '$1'. Check if it's installed and on your PATH.

column3: Disk is full — no free space
column5: Your disk has no space left. diskcheck shows which mounts are full.

column3: Service is not running or wrong port/host
column5: The target service refused the connection. Check if it's running with 'ports'.
